text.title: Claude API Core Rules
text.summary: 9 testable rules every direct-SDK Claude integration must satisfy. The validator enforces them mechanically; product prose cannot override.

rule.statement: Every messages.create call passes max_tokens explicitly: 512 for Haiku classification, 4096 for Sonnet generation, 8192+ for Opus extended reasoning.
rule.rationale: Default behaviour is model-dependent and changes between SDK versions; implicit defaults cause truncated outputs in production.

rule.statement: Any tool-use loop wraps the messages.create call in a for-range(MAX_TURNS) with default MAX_TURNS=10. On exhaustion the code raises RuntimeError carrying turn count and the conversation array.
rule.rationale: An agent without the cap loops indefinitely when Claude never returns stop_reason=end_turn — at Sonnet pricing a 1000-turn runaway costs ~$5 before hitting tier rate limits.

rule.statement: For JSON-shaped output use tool_choice={"type":"tool","name":"output_schema"} with a single output tool whose input_schema is the desired shape. Do not prompt for JSON in the user message.
rule.rationale: Forced tool use is the only contract-level guarantee of schema conformance across model updates; prompted JSON breaks silently after model upgrades.

rule.statement: cache_control:{"type":"ephemeral"} attaches to the longest stable prefix of the system+content array. All dynamic content (user turn, retrieved snippets, timestamps) appears AFTER the cached block.
rule.rationale: Cache matching is prefix-based — any change before the cached block invalidates the cache and forces a cache-write (1.25× input rate for 5-min TTL).

rule.statement: A block with cache_control set must be ≥1024 tokens (Sonnet/Opus) or ≥2048 tokens (Haiku family). Smaller blocks are silently not cached.
rule.rationale: Below the threshold the API accepts the cache_control header but does not cache; usage.cache_read_input_tokens stays 0 across all subsequent calls.

rule.statement: Extended Thinking (thinking={"type":"enabled","budget_tokens":N}) is enabled only when expected output is ≥200 tokens AND task is in {math, code-debugging, contract-analysis, multi-step-planning, research-synthesis}. Minimum budget=1024.

rationale.source: platform.claude.com/docs extended thinking; Opus 4.7 release notes

rule.rationale: Thinking tokens bill at output rate; the 1024-token minimum makes ET economically wasteful for classification, extraction, or short answers regardless of complexity claim.

rule.statement: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is loaded from environment or a secrets manager (1Password, AWS Secrets Manager, Vault). Source code, repo, and container images contain no literal key string.

rationale.source: OWASP secrets management; Anthropic key-rotation guidance

rule.rationale: Hardcoded keys leak via git history, container layers, CI logs, and crash dumps; once leaked they bill against the owning account until rotated.

rule.statement: Every API call is wrapped in retry-with-jitter: wait = min(2**attempt + random.uniform(0,1), 60). Max 5 attempts. Retry on RateLimitError, APIError where status_code >= 500, and APIConnectionError. Do not retry on 4xx other than 429.

rationale.source: Anthropic SDK retry guidance; tier rate-limit docs

rule.rationale: Tier rate limits (requests/min, tokens/min) trip quickly under parallel load; crash-on-first-429 wastes the in-flight conversation state.

rule.statement: Batch API (client.beta.messages.batches.create) is used only when no human is waiting on the result. Interactive chat, agent loops, and any UI-bound flow must use messages.create (sync or streaming).

rationale.source: platform.claude.com/docs Batch API; Anthropic Pricing 2026 batch entry

rule.rationale: Batch carries a 24-hour SLA and 50% discount; routing user-facing calls through it produces "the model is still thinking" stalls measured in hours, not seconds.
